observations: with_skill achieves 100% pass rate vs 93% without — the difference comes from ENS resolution which only the evm skill provides natively

token-info-base: with_skill was 2x faster (32s vs 70s) and used 20% fewer tokens (14.6k vs 18.4k) — without_skill wasted time trying Etherscan API first (which doesn't support Base) before falling back to raw RPC
contract-read: nearly identical performance — both tools (evm skill and etherscan skill) handle contract calls well
multi-chain-balance with_skill was slower (106s vs 40s) due to Ethereum RPC flakiness requiring retries — this is an RPC reliability issue, not a skill design issue
The evm skill's biggest advantage is on chains not covered by Etherscan (Base, Linea, Scroll, etc.) where without_skill has to improvise RPC calls from scratch
